description:
🕒 09:30–14:40

Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno Eco-Tourism Association: https://hanno-tourism.jp/hanno-eco/tour/tanetsuti-1-2/

⚠️ RESCHEDULED: The tour originally scheduled for June 21 (Sunday) has been moved to June 28 (Sunday) due to organizer circumstances.

Join this hands-on experience in a "kinchaku-hatake" (fermentation compost garden) that uses fallen leaves and branches. Learn bed-making techniques and how to plant and care for summer vegetable seedlings using the kinchaku farming method—advisors will explain tips in easy-to-understand detail. Lunch at the restaurant "La Nola" features Italian cuisine made with kinchaku vegetables.

【Fee】Adults ¥8,800 | Middle school students and younger ¥2,200 (includes guide, lunch, materials, insurance)

【Capacity】15 people

【Guide】Hanno Tanetotsuci Farm staff

【What to bring】Drinks, hat, towel, gloves, etc.
location: 集合 ラ・ノーラ前の畑 （飯能市下加治243-25、イーグルバス「飯能靖和病院」下車徒歩8分）
summary: Learn kinchaku-hatake (fermentation compost bed) gardening and plant summer vegetables in Hanno's forest setting
